Title: Biosynthesis and metabolic engineering of palmitoleate production, an important contributor to human health and sustainable industry

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes packaging/accumulation of palmitoleate into TAG; proposed engineering target to optimize high 16:1 storage
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGAT catalyzes the last step of TAG synthesis by adding a third acyl chain to DAG. 2. DGAT and particularly DGAT2 are proposed targets for optimizing accumulation of unusual fatty acids including palmitoleate in common oil crops. 3. Distinct ER-domain localization of DGATs may contribute to efficient unusual fatty acid channeling into storage oil.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of prior transgenic and biochemical studies; no single new experiment in this review
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: human health benefits; industrial feedstock for biodiesel improvement and 1-octene production
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it functions in acyl transfer/storage rather than as pathway catalyst directly defining palmitoleate synthesis step or transcriptional regulator.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Stack with Δ9 desaturases and elevated 16:0 background to improve transfer of palmitoleate into TAG in engineered oil crops.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Endogenous acyltransferase specificity may limit accumulation of unusual fatty acids in TAG.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed development (inferred from seed oil biosynthesis context)

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports transfer of acyl groups from PC to DAG for TAG formation, thereby potentially aiding palmitoleate accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDAT transfers the sn-2 acyl chain from PC to DAG to form TAG and lyso-PC. 2. PDAT overlaps functionally with DGAT in TAG synthesis in Arabidopsis seed and pollen. 3. Because palmitoleate synthesis/handling involves PC, PDAT is relevant to engineering its storage in TAG.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of prior genetic/biochemical studies
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: human health benefits; industrial oleochemical feedstock
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common gene involved in storage/packaging rather than direct synthesis or transcriptional regulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering unusual fatty acid accumulation in seed oil by improving flux from PC into TAG.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional redundancy with DGAT may complicate engineering outcomes.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: may enrich acyl-CoA pools with fatty acids synthesized on PC and thereby influence palmitoleate flux
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LPCAT provides a mechanism to enrich the acyl-CoA pool with fatty acids synthesized on PC. 2. This step is relevant because palmitoleate is synthesized and remodeled through PC intermediates. 3. LPCAT is a plausible engineering target for improving 16:1 movement from PC-derived pools into storage oil.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of prior biochemical studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core summarized common gene because the review emphasizes acyl editing and PC–acyl-CoA exchange as key to unusual fatty acid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for gene stacking with desaturases and DGAT/PDAT to optimize palmitoleate trafficking.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supplies DAG from PC and modulates fatty acid composition of TAG, thus potentially affecting palmitoleate accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDCT channels moieties from PC back into DAG for TAG synthesis. 2. Its substrate specificity helps direct different acyl species either toward desaturation or TAG assembly. 3. This makes PDCT relevant to engineering efficient accumulation of unusual fatty acids such as palmitoleate.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of prior biochemical studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common gene involved in intermediate exchange and storage-oil assembly.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to complement desaturase-based engineering when moving unusual fatty acids from PC to TAG.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates exported free fatty acids including 16:0 and 16:1 to acyl-CoAs, affecting availability for ER lipid metabolism
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACS converts exported free fatty acids to acyl-CoAs on the plastid outer membrane. 2. Different ACS isoforms can show substrate preferences. 3. Such specificity could influence the amount of palmitoleate entering ER glycerolipid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of prior enzymology and substrate-specificity studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common gene because it supplies activated substrates rather than serving as the highlighted desaturation step.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Selection or engineering of ACSs with favorable 16:1 preference could improve metabolic flux toward palmitoleate-rich oil.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: can favor release/export of 16:1-ACP, supporting palmitoleate accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FatA generally prefers unsaturated acyl-ACPs. 2. Macadamia FatA and castor FatA were reported to show high specificity toward palmitoleoyl-ACP. 3. Therefore FatA is proposed as a target to improve palmitoleate metabolic engineering.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of substrate-specificity enzyme studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common gene because it releases fatty acids from ACP for export rather than introducing the double bond itself.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineer or introduce FatA alleles with strong 16:1-ACP preference to enhance palmitoleate export from plastids.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: preferentially releases saturated acyl-ACPs such as 16:0, affecting precursor partitioning away from or toward palmitoleate production depending on context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FatB is generally more specific for saturated acyl-ACPs such as 16:0. 2. Macadamia FatB showed lower specificity to 16:1 than FatA, and castor FatB preferred 16:0-ACP. 3. Manipulating FatB may alter precursor competition in palmitoleate engineering strategies.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of substrate-specificity enzyme studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common gene influencing precursor release rather than direct desaturation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Down-tuning saturated-acyl-preferring FatB or replacing it with favorable thioesterase combinations may improve 16:1 yield.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Viridiplantae; Fungi; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Rattus norvegicus
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid acyl-ACP desaturation and ER acyl-CoA/acyl-PC desaturation branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces a cis Δ9 double bond into palmitate to convert 16:0 to palmitoleate (16:1Δ9)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for palmitoleate synthesis emphasized throughout review
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: soluble acyl-ACP desaturase or membrane-bound acyl-CoA desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: plastid stroma or endoplasmic reticulum depending on enzyme type
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increasing Δ9D activity enhances palmitoleate biosynthesis, but expression alone usually yields ≤10% 16:1 in transgenic plants unless combined with additional modifications
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Δ9 desaturase is the major target for engineering palmitoleate biosynthesis. 2. Heterologous expression from yeast, mammals, and high-16:1 plants increases 16:1 in multiple transgenic hosts. 3. Expression of Δ9D alone generally gives only moderate accumulation, indicating need for multi-gene engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; transgenic metabolite profiling; positional lipid analysis; western blot for localization; seed-specific expression studies
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed oil TAG; also membrane polar lipids in leaves/tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central enzyme for palmitoleate engineering; best deployed with elevated 16:0 supply and improved TAG-packaging machinery.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes both ACP-dependent and CoA-dependent Δ9 desaturases; gene-specific examples listed separately below where sufficiently focal.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: parallel plastid desaturation step affecting precursor partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 18:0-ACP; also discussed in context of elevated desaturase activity linked to high omega-7 phenotype
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: indirectly affects precursor balance and omega-7 phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: fab1 mutant background elevates 16:0 substrate and when combined with Com25 strongly increases omega-7 accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The fab1 elevated-16:0 mutant has reduced KASII activity and doubles 16:0 in Arabidopsis seeds. 2. Expression of Com25 in fab1 lines increased omega-7 fatty acids to ~39%. 3. FAB1-associated genetic background is thus important for maximizing engineered palmitoleate production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; transgenic expression in mutant background; seed fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In figure legend FAB1 is defined as stearoyl-ACP desaturase 1, but in engineering section 'fab1' is treated as the elevated-16:0 mutant background linked to reduced KASII; review wording is internally inconsistent, so effect recorded as described.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use elevated-16:0 fab1 background with introduced 16:0-specific desaturase to boost palmitoleate synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Glycine max; Arabidopsis thaliana; Helianthus annuus
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: elongates palmitoyl-ACP to stearoyl-ACP in fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies or limits precursor pool for target product by competing with 16:0 desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key precursor-partitioning control point for engineering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduction or mutation of KASII elevates 16:0 substrate and thereby facilitates higher palmitoleate production when coupled to Δ9 desaturase activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KASII converts 16:0-ACP to 18:0-ACP, competing with 16:0 desaturation to palmitoleate. 2. Soybean fap2 mutation in GmKASIIA causes elevated 16:0. 3. Reduced KASII activity in Arabidopsis/sunflower backgrounds enhances omega-7 engineering potential by increasing precursor availability.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant identification; sequence lesion mapping; breeding; seed fatty acid profiling; combinatorial transgenic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing KASII is a major strategy to increase 16:0 substrate for introduced 16:0-specific Δ9 desaturases.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In sunflower, high omega-7 accumulation was associated with decreased oil yield.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A direct palmitoleate enzyme target only indirectly; included because the review strongly emphasizes it as a core engineering node.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with target-product formation by consuming 16:0 substrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: premature stop mutation in GmKASIIA elevates 16:0 and is proposed as a host background to support higher transgenic palmitoleate production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The soybean fap2 allele contains a single base substitution in GmKASIIA creating a premature stop codon. 2. Reduced elongation of 16:0-ACP likely caused elevated seed palmitate. 3. This mutant is proposed as a useful transgenic host to increase 16:1 production by introduced Δ9 desaturases.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutation mapping and fatty acid phenotype analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Deploy as a precursor-enhanced soybean background for palmitoleate engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High 16:0 background may alter oil quality if desaturation is insufficient.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: engineered 18:0Δ9-ACP desaturase with enhanced 16:0-ACP desaturase activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: engineered soluble desaturase optimized to introduce a Δ9 double bond into 16:0-ACP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: seed-specific expression markedly increases omega-7 fatty acids, especially when combined with elevated 16:0 mutants and ER acyl-CoA desaturases
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Com25 was generated by enzyme evolution from a castor desaturase to improve 16:0-ACP desaturase activity. 2. In Arabidopsis seeds it produced ~14% omega-7 fatty acids alone. 3. In fab1/fae1 elevated-16:0 backgrounds plus ER desaturases, omega-7 fatty acids reached up to ~67%, including ~43% palmitoleate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme evolution; seed-specific transgenic expression; combinatorial engineering; seed fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly valuable engineered enzyme for commercial palmitoleate production when stacked with precursor-enhancing and ER-desaturase genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the clearest focal pathway genes in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: elongates cis-vaccenic acid (18:1Δ11) to eicosenoic acid (20:1Δ13)
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with retention of palmitoleate-derived omega-7 intermediates by elongating them
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: fae1 mutation helps retain more omega-7 fatty acids in shorter-chain forms and contributes to elevated precursor pools in combinatorial engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAE1 elongates 18:1Δ11 to 20:1Δ13 in the cytoplasm. 2. The fae1 mutant was combined with fab1 to increase 16:0 and optimize omega-7 accumulation. 3. Expression of Com25 in the double mutant produced ~50% omega-7 fatty acids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant combination and transgenic expression with fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful background allele for stacking with plastid and ER desaturases to increase palmitoleate-rich omega-7 oils.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: desaturates oleic acid on PC to linoleic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: indirect competing branch within seed oil unsaturated fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: contextual enzyme in PC-centered fatty acid remodeling pathway relevant to unusual fatty acid flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD2 is part of the PC desaturation network in the ER. 2. PC is a central intermediate in unusual fatty acid metabolism including palmitoleate handling. 3. FAD2 represents a competing endogenous activity that may influence flux distribution in engineered oils.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because explicitly highlighted in pathway schematic and discussion of flux through PC, though not a primary engineering focal gene in this review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: desaturates linoleic acid to linolenic acid on PC
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: indirect competing branch within seed oil desaturation network
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: contextual competing enzyme influencing acyl flux through PC during oil biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD3 acts sequentially after FAD2 in ER lipid desaturation. 2. The PC intermediate pool central to palmitoleate engineering is also a substrate for FAD3-driven polyunsaturation. 3. This places FAD3 in the broader flux network affecting engineered oil composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background-to-core borderline; included because explicitly summarized in the biosynthetic framework of the review.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: controls oil biosynthesis broadly and is suggested as part of the regulatory framework relevant to palmitoleate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRI1 is cited as a transcription factor controlling oil biosynthesis. 2. Regulation of oil synthesis occurs at multiple levels and likely impacts palmitoleate accumulation. 3. Mechanistic details specific to 16:1 regulation remain poorly understood.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review citation of prior functional studies
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: broad upstream controller of seed oil synthesis, but palmitoleate-specific control is not established
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only regulator explicitly named in the review; relationship to palmitoleate is indirect and general rather than gene-specific for 16:1.
